notes: Always warm up for 5-10 minutes before starting
Stay hydrated throughout your workout
If any exercise causes pain, stop immediately
Focus on progressive overload - increase weight or reps each week

exercises.name: decline push-up
notes: Place your hands on the ground slightly wider than shoulder-width apart, with your feet elevated on a stable surface.
Keep your body in a straight line from head to toe, engaging your core muscles.
Lower your chest towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your body.
Push through your palms to extend your arms and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

exercises.name: Band Triceps Lunge
notes: Stand in a stable position preparing to perform the 'Band Triceps Lunge'.
Grab the skierg machine securely and set up your stance according to proper form.
Execute the main movement: focus on controlled motion targeting the triceps.
Maintain a controlled tempo, exhale during the exertion phase and inhale during the return.
Keep a neutral spine, engage your core, and avoid locking joints at full extension.
Repeat for desired repetitions, starting with 8–12 reps for general strength or adjust as needed.
Secondary muscles engaged: wrist extensors.
After finishing, perform a brief cool-down and stretch the worked muscles.

exercises.name: Advanced Forearms Press

Secondary muscles engaged: trapezius.
After finishing, perform a brief cool-down and stretch the worked muscles.

exercises.name: front plank with twist
notes: Start in a high plank position with your hands directly under your shoulders and your body in a straight line from head to toe.
Engage your core and glutes to maintain a stable position.
Rotate your torso to the right, lifting your right arm and extending it towards the ceiling.
Keep your hips and legs stable as you twist.
Hold for a moment, then return to the starting position.
Repeat the twist on the left side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

weeks.notes: Week 1: Focus on form and progressive overload.
weeks.theme: Foundation - Focus on form and baseline
